snippet.title: iPhone 17 or 17 Pro: Watch This Before You Buy
snippet.description:
So, it's been about a month. I've been daily driving the iPhone 17 Pro (Silver) and my secondary, the iPhone 17 (Lavender). After 30 days and over 20 charge cycles, I've got a lot of thoughts.

This year, the upgrade decision is harder than ever... because the base model iPhone 17 finally got the 120Hz ProMotion display we've all been asking for. It finally feels "Pro."

But the 17 Pro has its own massive upgrades, like a new vapor chamber for thermals, and... some new controversies, like "scratchgate" and the switch back to aluminum.

In this video, we're covering my one-month-later review to help you decide if you should buy or upgrade. We'll talk:

Design & Display (The new 120Hz base model is a game-changer)
iPhone 17 vs 17 Pro Performance (A19 vs. A19 Pro... with Geekbench scores)
'Scratchgate' & The Pro's New Aluminum Body
The Vapor Chamber (Why this is the real pro feature)
New Cameras (That new front camera is... different)
Who should buy the 17 vs. 17 Pro?

If you're coming from an iPhone 13, 14, 15, or even 16, this is the review to watch before you spend your money.

What do you think of the new camera 'plateau'? Is 'scratchgate' a dealbreaker for you? Let's talk in the comments!

snippet.title: Đánh giá chi tiết iPhone 17 và iPhone 17 Pro
snippet.description:
Đánh giá & So sánh chi tiết iPhone 17 và iPhone 17 Pro

Tham khảo giá bán của iPhone 17 và iPhone 17 Pro cùng chương trình khuyến mại tại Thegioididong và TopZone:
►  Thegioididong: https://www.thegioididong.com/dtdd-apple-iphone-17-series?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=vvs-mota-ytvx&utm_campaign=media-VVS-251115171352262&utm_content=media-VVS-danhgiachitietiphone-251115171352262
► TopZone: https://www.topzone.vn/iphone-17?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=vvs-mota-ytvx&utm_campaign=media-VVS-251115171437964&utm_content=media-VVS-danhgiachitietiphone-251115171437964

► Trong video này, mình sẽ chia sẻ đánh giá iPhone 17 và iPhone 17 Pro để anh em dễ hình dung sự khác biệt thực tế giữa hai mẫu máy đang cách nhau gần 10 triệu. Cả hai đều có nhiều điểm giống nhau như màn hình 120Hz, độ sáng cao, thiết kế khung nhôm, chip A19 và camera chính 48MP. Tuy nhiên, iPhone 17 Pro vẫn hướng đến nhóm người dùng cần nhiều sức mạnh hơn với chip A19 Pro, RAM lớn hơn, tản nhiệt tốt hơn và cụm 3 camera đầy đủ cùng tele zoom quang 4x–8x. Ngoài ra, bản Pro còn có USB 3.0, quay ProRes, LIDAR và thiết kế cao cấp hơn. snippet.title: Ringke Fusion iPhone 17 Pro Max Review - A $16 Case That Performs Way Above Its Price
snippet.description:
You get what you pay for… right? That’s what I assumed going into the Ringke Fusion, a $16 clear case that looks as basic as clear cases get. But after running it through 30 drops, face-first impacts, and camera-lump hits, this tiny budget case performed way better than I expected.

Get your own Ringke Fusion for iPhone 17 Pro Max Case, [Compatible with MagSafe] UV Coating for Scratch & Discoloration Resistant, Shockproof Air Pockets - Magnetic Clear by using this link: 
*This is an Amazon affiliate link that will bring you to the right Amazon store for your location

#ReviewerNotInfluencer #Reviews #iPhone

In this video:
0:00 Intro
0:28 Design
0:54 Handling & Usability
1:36 Protection Features
2:15 Drop Protection
3:57 Should You Get It?

Ringke Fusion for iPhone 17 Pro Max Case, [Compatible with MagSafe] UV Coating for Scratch & Discoloration Resistant, Shockproof Air Pockets - Magnetic Clear Review

-if there was a floor for clear cases in terms of design
-this is it
-typical TPU PC clear case
-fingerprint magnet
-couple of nice design features
-corners are higher for better screen clearance
-feet on the back for scratch protection
-case is super slick despite being a TPU case
-it feels super cheap
-this is a tiny case, rivaling the size of Apple's cases
-only weight 36g
-increases the bulk of your iPhone certain places by 58%
-now despite being a tiny case
-in my corner drop tests, the iPhone felt 9.2G's which is 12% better than average
-the rotation of the iPhone coming off the drop was basically average
-the settling rate was average
-and because of the blunted initial impact, it was 17% better than average

-when it comes to face first protection
-well, this tiny case
-passed
-initial impact was only 2.5G's, that's 36% better than average
-it settled 30% faster
-and the total force going into the iPhone was 11% better

-for the corner lump, instead of having a massive TPU bumper
-Ringke went with having bumpers around each individual lens
-in my drop tests, the intial drop was 16% better
-setlling rate was 35% better
-overall impulse was 12% better

-so this absolute tiny thing of a case has amazing drop test numbers
-BUT here's the thing

-my corner drops are set at 36 inches in height
-to simulate coming off a desk, counter or out of your pockets
-every case that passes in my test group at 36 inches goes to 48 inches or chest height
-at 48 inches, the intial impact on the iPhone after 10 drops is around 10G's. 10% higher
-the average so far in that test group is 9G's
-so anything above a pocket drop for this case is going to be ok
-12 inches higher, the corners start bottoming out

-back thickness is a paltry 2mm
-corner thickness is only 1.4mm
-case has sizable air pockets
-corners ate their thinnest are only .7mm thick which is wild
-when it comes to rigidity, this case only takes 18.6N to push 8mm
-that's 35% below average

-only saving grace for durability is the lack of sliding issues
-for magsafe, took 8.6N to pull, 8.9 to push
-this is 11% below average 
-I'd be wary about using a car mount accessory with it
-buttons on the case are integrated and stiff
-camera cutout button is non-existent
-port cutout is rough
-the screen access is great because of the super low edges
